title: Game Evangelion PC games
wikitext:
[]: [Game] Evangelion PC games             Quote Post by shiun » Wed Jul 04, 2012 8:58 pm Hey guys! I'd like to know more Eva PC games, whether they are simple Flash games, doujin games, console games played via emulator, the original PC games, etc. What are available right now, translated to English, or just in Japanese but are still enjoyable to play? I'm actually leaning to finding out more about the Evangelion 2 PS2/PSP game and if a PC copy of it exists right now. If so, what are your experiences with it [s:zr7s4r89]and where can I get the best copy lol[/s:zr7s4r89]? A quick Google search says that it exists, but I'm totally clueless when it comes to looking for and playing PC games and have no idea where to start. I just like RPG games more and Eva 2 seemed up my alley. Give me all you got though! I'm just really bored at the moment and want to delve into something Eva-related.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jul 04, 2012 10:39 pm There isn't a single Evangelion game that has gotten an English release. Girlfriend of Steel has an English fan-translation though.

[]:             Quote Post by shiun » Wed Jul 04, 2012 10:47 pm Awww man, that sucks. I'm guessing none of the other games have fan-translations either? Why must it be so hard to find translated Eva material WHYYYY This needs thorough investigation.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Thu Jul 05, 2012 3:27 am http://swfchan.com/22/107191/?NeoTokyoIIITaisen.swf I made a shitty eva flash game based off some open-source space invaders some time ago

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jul 05, 2012 8:27 am shiun wrote: Awww man, that sucks. I'm guessing none of the other games have fan-translations either? Why must it be so hard to find translated Eva material WHYYYY This needs thorough investigation.

[]:             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Thu Jul 05, 2012 11:45 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Also, Evangelion games are kind of "meh" from what I've heard. They're not as good as you would think they would be.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Sat Jul 07, 2012 9:30 am Could somebody possibly find or make a English translation of "Stripping Instrumentality Project" please? Ignore the naked parts I would honestly just like a translation of the comic panels with Gunbuster and Eva really. Link to pornographic materials removed, PM Great Genius Shinji-Sama for link

[]:             Quote Post by Redaye » Sun Jul 08, 2012 9:30 pm I'm sure I've mentioned this in anouther thread, but I think EVA would make a fantastic MMOFPS. Core it up on the Quake engine and painted by Weta Workshop, it would have a home in the west.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Jul 09, 2012 9:37 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by Redaye » Mon Jul 09, 2012 10:14 am Seele00TextOnly wrote: Redaye: Still stuck on 'position the target in the center, pull the switch' eh?

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Mon Jul 09, 2012 11:15 am Erm, Sorry about that Like I said, I wasn't after pr0n, but I just wanted a translation for the EvaxGunbuster parts from Shinji and Good Friends. I guess I could have found a better link.

[]:             Quote Post by Unit03 » Fri Jul 03, 2015 8:30 pm What about something like a eva mmofps pc game campaign/mmo were you get to make your own customized eva and either fight angles or evas. if someone agreed to make this a reality i don't know what i would do. Evangelion deserves a good pc game

[]:             Quote Post by deeRez » Fri Jul 03, 2015 9:29 pm While I'd love to get off topic and talk about what the perfectly designed Eva game would look like, or about the hilarity behind men being turned on by 2D pictures of women ... drawn by men, on topic: There was a neat PC strategy game hosted at evangelion.tv (site went defunct in 2007), called "Angel Song". If I remember it correctly, it was very addicting. Sadly archive.org only archived the site AFTER they 404'd the files to fight leechers. [url]https://web.archive.org/web/20010322222802/http://www.evangelion.tv/Multi/games2.html[/url] * EDIT * - Found it back in time: http://web.archive.org/web/20010611030029/http://toget.pchome.com.tw/intro/game_simulation/game_simulation_war/38.html Download still works if you trim out the url contained in Soft_url=, but I'm not going to run it. First I need to setup a VM cause I don't trust random exes I find on the web. * EDIT * - Turns out the game is official and came from: [url]http://wiki.evageeks.org/Neon_Genesis_Evangelion_Collector's_Discs[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sat Jul 04, 2015 2:09 am The Eva Monkey wrote: No, as far as I know, GOS is the only one to receive an English translation. Basically, it's a numbers game. Evangelion video games do not appeal to mainstream gamers AT ALL, and the number of Evangelion fans who are gamers, who would play an Evangelion video game are not numerous enough to merit license and distribution. It's not like Dragon Ball Z fighting games where it's much easier to localize, because there's much less translation, and the games appeal to a broader fighting game market. There are plenty of people who are not fans of DBZ, Naruto, or Gundam who would play games based on them, because they have more appeal, and they also make better games. Also, Evangelion games are kind of "meh" from what I've heard. They're not as good as you would think they would be.
